business_context: The Retail Banking division is undergoing a digital transformation initiative to modernize its customer-facing payment infrastructure. The current PayHub platform processes over 2 million transactions daily but lacks real-time fraud detection and cross-channel reconciliation capabilities, resulting in elevated dispute rates and manual intervention costs.
problem_statement: Customers and internal operations teams face significant friction due to payment processing delays, limited transaction visibility, and a fragmented reconciliation process across channels. This results in increased customer churn, higher operational costs, and regulatory exposure in markets requiring same-day settlement.

context_map.detail: Unified real-time payment API layer integrating with PayHub, supporting web, mobile, and partner integrations
Retail customers (individual account holders), SME clients, and internal operations teams
No real-time fraud scoring at transaction ingestion; batch-only reconciliation creates 24-48h settlement lag
Regulatory mandate for ISO 20022 compliance by Q4 2026; competitive pressure from fintech entrants
Reduce payment dispute rate by 40% and cut reconciliation cycle time from 48h to under 4h

open_questions.question: Which specific payment rails (SEPA, SWIFT, domestic ACH) are in scope for the real-time layer?
open_questions.why: Needed to scope integration complexity and compliance requirements
open_questions.example: e.g. 'SEPA Credit Transfer and domestic Faster Payments are in scope; SWIFT cross-border is out of scope for Phase 1'

open_questions.question: Is there a hard regulatory deadline for ISO 20022 adoption or is it phased?
open_questions.why: Affects milestone planning and risk prioritization
open_questions.example: e.g. 'Hard deadline: 30 Nov 2026 for all new outgoing settlement messages; existing messages get a 6-month grace period'
